rules.title: Axe and tree interaction
rules.text: Trees block movement. The player cannot walk onto a tree tile. A tree can only be cut if the player has an axe selected and is directly adjacent to the tree. Use the weapon in the direction of the adjacent tree.

rules.title: Door and corridor traversal
rules.text: Closed doors and open doors are entered by moving onto the exact visible door tile. After stepping onto a door, the game automatically traverses the corridor. Do not plan additional actions after entering a door because a new room observation is needed.

rules.title: Cobweb hazard
rules.text: Cobweb tiles are walkable hazard tiles that can trap the player. Prefer ordinary O floor tiles when a safe route exists. It is acceptable to step onto a cobweb if it is the only practical route to reach an axe, a door, or a tree-adjacent attack tile, or if avoiding it would cause excessive backtracking. If the player is stuck on a cobweb, repeat the same movement direction up to 4 times to break free; do not choose a different route until free. Do not repeatedly move onto new cobweb tiles unless necessary.

rules.title: Chess knight enemy
rules.text: A chess knight attacks tiles in an L shape: two tiles in one axis and one tile in the other axis. Avoid tiles attacked by a knight even if those tiles look walkable.

rules.title: Exploration priority
rules.text: If the needed object is not visible and not known in memory, explore through unexplored doors. Prefer doors that do not already have known room connections.
